title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Rory informs Lynn that he consulted Hercule Poirot, who identified a man named Potter as the best friend of Robert Andraud. Potter confirmed that the deceased man at Stagg was indeed Robert Andraud. Rory celebrates this discovery, claiming it defeats the "con artists" Hunter and his sister. He explains that because Rosalind was not married to Gordon when her first husband was alive, she cannot claim Gordon's money, meaning Rory will receive a quarter of the inheritance. Rory suggests providing Rosalind with a living allowance, though he expresses contempt for David, accusing him of being a murderer.

Rory believes David killed André to prevent blackmail and to secure the inheritance. However, Lynn counters his timeline by noting that David caught the 9:20 train to London, which she knows because he called her from there. Despite her defense, Lynn privately contemplates whether David's strange behavior and haste suggest he killed André before catching the train. She fears that David will eventually be hanged.

In a separate scene, David instructs a distressed Rosalind to remain calm and stick to their story: that the dead man was not her husband, Robert Andraud. Rosalind expresses guilt, fearing divine punishment for taking money that does not belong to them. David uses fear to manipulate her, telling her that only her admission of the truth could lead to his execution. He advises her to act "a little stupid" during questioning and informs her that he has hired a criminal lawyer, Guy Thorn, to protect her during the interrogation.
